hub_subtitle: Inglês para o trabalho — do aperto de mão ao acordo fechado, em 7 aulas

lesson.menu_desc: Describe your operation in numbers to an international buyer -- read an American farm profile and compare -- 33 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 2 -- My Farm, My Numbers
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ziláudio | Lesson 2 -- My Farm, My Numbers

listenings.text: Hi Zilaudio, this is David from the trading company in Chicago. Thank you again for the call last week. My team wants to understand your operation better, so I have a few questions about the numbers. How many hectares does the farm have in total? How much soybean do you produce in a good season? And how many trucks do you use to move the crop from the fields to the port? One more thing: do you have your own storage on the farm, or do you rent a silo? If you can send me these numbers by email, that helps us a lot. Thank you, and talk to you soon.

Good morning, and here is the farm report for this week. The weather stays dry across Mato Grosso, with strong sun from Monday to Thursday. A little rain arrives on Friday, and that is good news for the fields. The soybean price goes up again this week, so it is a good moment to sell. Buyers in China want more, and the trucks are busy on the roads to the port. For the next season, the experts say the yield will be high, if the rain comes at the right time. That is the report. Have a good week on the farm.

paras: John Miller is a farmer in Iowa, in the center of the United States. His farm is big, but it is not as big as a farm in Mato Grosso. It covers two thousand acres. On his land, he grows corn and soybeans, and the farm produces a lot of food every year.

In a good season, the yield is high. John has modern machinery -- three tractors and two big harvesters. From September to November, the machines work every day, from morning to night. The weather is the big question: too much rain, or too little rain, and the yield goes down.

After the harvest, John keeps the corn in two tall silos near the house. He has storage for four thousand tons. Then the trucks come. Every week, ten trucks carry the corn from the farm to the river. "My farm is small for the United States," John says, "but the numbers still tell the story."
reading.source: English for Business & Agribusiness -- profile written for Ziláudio's package

reading.prompt: What is the best title for this text?
choices: Why Iowa Is Bigger Than Brazil

items: John's farm is bigger than a farm in Mato Grosso.
"His farm is big, but it is not as big as a farm in Mato Grosso."
John grows corn and soybeans on his land.
"On his land, he grows corn and soybeans."
The machines work from September to November.
"From September to November, the machines work every day."
John has storage for four hundred tons.
"He has storage for four thousand tons -- not four hundred."

bank.label: How to describe your farm in numbers
items: The farm has two thousand hectares.
It produces about fifty tons of soybean per hectare.
How many trucks do you use?
How much rain do you get in the season?
We store the soybean in two silos.
The harvest runs from January to March.

items.situation: A buyer asks: "How big is your farm?" Answer with the number.
tips: The farm has two thousand hectares.
Number + unit: two thousand hectares, no -s on the number.

items.situation: The buyer asks: "How many silos do you have?" You can count silos, so...
tips: We have two silos on the farm.
Count it -> how many -> a plain number.

items.situation: He asks: "How much soybean do you produce in a season?" Answer with tons.
tips: We produce about fifty tons per hectare.
You cannot count soybean like that -> how much.

items.situation: He asks: "When is the harvest?" Use from ... to.
tips: The harvest runs from January to March.

items.situation: He asks: "How do you move the crop?" Say how many trucks, and to where.
tips: Ten trucks carry the crop from the farm to the port.
from the farm to the port.

items.situation: Close the call. Say you will send the numbers by email.
tips: I will send you all the numbers by email.
